days.title: Arrival in Lisbon
days.subtitle: First taste of Portuguese culture

timeline.title: Check-in at Hotel Lisboa Plaza
timeline.detail: Arrive at Hotel Lisboa Plaza, where you will check in and refresh yourself. The hotel features elegant decor and is centrally located, making it ideal for your adventure.

timeline.title: Explore the Alfama District
timeline.detail: Wander through the charming narrow streets of the Alfama district. This area is renowned for its history, architecture, and vibrant atmosphere that reflects traditional Portuguese culture.

timeline.title: Lunch: Traditional Portuguese Meal
timeline.detail: Stop for lunch at a local restaurant specializing in Portuguese cuisine. Savor classic dishes made with fresh, local ingredients, providing insight into the region's culinary heritage.

timeline.title: Fado Music Experience
timeline.detail: Attend a Fado music performance in a local venue. This soulful genre embodies the emotion of Portuguese culture, often sung in intimate settings to enhance the experience.
timeline.mapQuery: Fado music venue, Alfama, Lisbon, Portugal

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: Miradouro de Santa Luzia
timeline.detail: This viewpoint offers stunning views over the Alfama district and the Tagus River, perfect for capturing the essence of Lisbon. The panoramic view is breathtaking.
timeline.mapQuery: Miradouro de Santa Luzia, Lisbon, Portugal
timeline.tip: Best angle: stand at the edge of the terrace. Shoot toward the river. Best light: golden hour. Include the rooftops in the foreground.

timeline.title: Taste Pastéis de Belém
timeline.detail: Indulge in the famous Pastéis de Belém at the original bakery. These delicious custard tarts are a must-try when visiting Lisbon and are served warm with cinnamon.

timeline.title: Dinner: Seafood Feast
timeline.detail: Enjoy a seafood dinner at a local restaurant known for its fresh catches. Dine on dishes like grilled sardines or octopus, accompanied by local wines for an authentic taste of Lisbon.

timeline.title: Evening stroll along the Tagus River
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely evening stroll along the Tagus River. This serene walk is a perfect way to end your day, offering calming views and a chance to reflect on your experiences.

localTips: Purchase a 24-hour public transport pass for unlimited rides.
Visit Belém early to avoid queues at the pastry shop.
Explore the Alfama district by foot for authentic local vibes.

days.title: Exploring Lisbon's History
days.subtitle: Dive into the past and cultural richness

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel Lisboa Plaza
timeline.detail: Start your day with a delicious breakfast buffet featuring local pastries, cheeses, and fresh fruits. Enjoy a relaxing atmosphere to fuel up for a busy day exploring Lisbon.

timeline.title: Transport to Castelo de São Jorge
timeline.detail: Take the metro from Avenida to Rossio and then a scenic walk up to Castelo de São Jorge. This castle, offering stunning views of the city, dates back to the medieval period.

timeline.title: Explore Castelo de São Jorge
timeline.detail: Visit the historic Castelo de São Jorge, where you can roam through its ancient walls and enjoy panoramic views of Lisbon. Learn about its fascinating history and significance to the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Castelo de São Jorge, Lisbon, Portugal

timeline.title: Lunch at Time Out Market
timeline.detail: Head to the Time Out Market for a unique lunch experience. Sample a variety of dishes from iconic Portuguese chefs and food stalls, all in a vibrant, bustling atmosphere.

timeline.title: Transport to Lisbon Cathedral
timeline.detail: After lunch, take a leisurely stroll to the Lisbon Cathedral, also known as Sé de Lisboa. Enjoy the sights along the way as you soak in the local culture and atmosphere.

timeline.title: Visit Lisbon Cathedral
timeline.detail: Explore Lisbon Cathedral, a stunning example of Romanesque architecture. Admire its beautiful stained glass windows and intricate designs while learning about its rich history and significance.

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: View from Miradouro de Santa Luzia
timeline.detail: This viewpoint offers breathtaking views over the Alfama district and the Tagus River. Capture stunning photos with the colorful tiles and the river in the background.
timeline.mapQuery: Miradouro de Santa Luzia, Lisbon, Portugal
timeline.tip: Best angle: stand by the railing. Shoot toward the river and castle. Best light: late afternoon for softer tones.

timeline.title: Transport back to Hotel Lisboa Plaza
timeline.detail: Take the tram from the nearby stop back to your hotel. Use this time to relax and reflect on your inspiring day filled with culture and history.

timeline.title: Dinner at a Local Restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy a leisurely dinner at a local Portuguese restaurant. Indulge in traditional dishes such as Bacalhau à Brás or Pastéis de Nata for dessert, rounding off your culinary adventure.

timeline.title: Relaxing Evening at Hotel
timeline.detail: Return to Hotel Lisboa Plaza and unwind. Enjoy the hotel's amenities, perhaps with a nightcap at the bar or some light reading in a cozy lounge area before bed.

localTips: Book tickets online for the castle to skip the line.
Try the food market for a mix of local dishes.
Be mindful of pickpockets in crowded areas.

days.title: Day Trip to Sintra
days.subtitle: Discover fairy-tale palaces and lush gardens

timeline.title: Hotel: Breakfast at Hotel Lisboa Plaza
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delightful buffet breakfast at Hotel Lisboa Plaza to start your day. The menu includes a variety of pastries, fresh fruits, and local specialties. Make sure to fuel up for your trip to Sintra!

timeline.title: Transport: Train to Sintra
timeline.detail: Catch the train from Rossio Station to Sintra, a scenic journey of approximately 40 minutes. Make sure to validate your ticket before boarding to avoid fines. Enjoy the beautiful views of the countryside during the ride.

timeline.title: Activity: Visit Pena Palace
timeline.detail: Marvel at the stunning architecture and vibrant colors of Pena Palace. This 19th-century Romanticist castle is a UNESCO World Heritage site and offers breathtaking views of the surrounding hills. Explore the gardens and learn about the history.

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: View from Pena Palace
timeline.detail: From the terrace of Pena Palace, witness panoramic views of Sintra's lush landscape. The vibrant colors of the palace against the green hills make for stunning photographs.
timeline.tip: Best angle: stand on the terrace facing north. Shoot toward the hills. Best light: morning light enhances colors. Include the palace in foreground.

timeline.title: Meal: Lunch at Tascardoso
timeline.detail: Savor local Portuguese cuisine at Tascardoso, known for its traditional dishes and cozy atmosphere. Try the seafood rice and the house wine for an authentic Sintra experience.

timeline.title: Activity: Explore Quinta da Regaleira
timeline.detail: Discover the enchanting gardens and mystical architecture of Quinta da Regaleira. The initiation wells and hidden tunnels offer a magical experience. Take your time to wander through the gardens and soak in the mystical ambiance.

timeline.title: Transport: Return to Lisbon
timeline.detail: Head back to Sintra Station and catch the train back to Lisbon. The journey gives you time to relax and reflect on your adventure in the fairy-tale town of Sintra.

timeline.title: Activity: Evening stroll in Lisbon
timeline.detail: Once back in Lisbon, enjoy a leisurely evening stroll through the historic streets of the city. Visit the lively Chiado district, filled with shops, cafes, and beautiful squares as the city comes alive at night.

timeline.title: Meal: Dinner at Time Out Market
timeline.detail: End your day with dinner at Time Out Market, where you can sample various dishes from some of Lisbon's best chefs. Enjoy the vibrant atmosphere and try different bites of local cuisines all in one place.

timeline.title: Hotel: Return to Hotel Lisboa Plaza
timeline.detail: Return to your comfortable room at Hotel Lisboa Plaza to unwind after a busy day. Take some time to reflect on the experiences had in Sintra and recharge for tomorrow's adventures.

localTips: Take an early train to avoid the crowds at the palace.
Wear comfortable shoes for walking around the gardens.
Try the local Travesseiros pastry while in Sintra.

days.title: Final Day in Lisbon
days.subtitle: Relaxation and last explorations

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel Lisboa Plaza
timeline.detail: Enjoy a relaxing breakfast at the hotel’s buffet, featuring fresh pastries, local cheeses, fruits, and freshly brewed coffee to fuel your day ahead.

timeline.title: Transport to Cascais
timeline.detail: Take the scenic train from Cais do Sodré station to Cascais. The ride lasts about 40 minutes and offers beautiful views of the coastline.
timeline.mapQuery: Cais do Sodré Train Station, Lisbon, Portugal

timeline.title: Relax at Cascais Beach
timeline.detail: Arrive at Cascais beach and enjoy the sun and surf. Whether you take a stroll along the shore or relax on the soft sand, it’s a perfect escape.

timeline.title: Lunch at Casa da Guia
timeline.detail: Savor a leisurely lunch at Casa da Guia, a charming seaside restaurant with views of the ocean. Enjoy fresh seafood dishes and local specialties.

timeline.title: Explore Modern Lisbon
timeline.detail: After returning to Lisbon, explore the modern architecture of the Parque das Nações area. Visit the Oceanário and walk along the beautiful waterfront.

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: Vasco da Gama Bridge
timeline.detail: Capture stunning views of the Vasco da Gama Bridge from the waterfront. The bridge is one of the longest in Europe and offers fantastic photo opportunities.
timeline.mapQuery: Vasco da Gama Bridge, Lisbon, Portugal
timeline.tip: Best angle: from the riverside park. Shoot toward the bridge. Best light: late afternoon/sunset to enhance the colors.

timeline.title: Dinner at Time Out Market
timeline.detail: Enjoy a farewell dinner at the famous Time Out Market, where you can choose from various food stalls featuring some of Lisbon’s best chefs and local dishes.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel Lisboa Plaza
timeline.detail: After dinner, take a leisurely stroll back to your hotel, reflecting on your trip and perhaps stopping for an evening gelato along the way.

timeline.title: Prepare for Departure
timeline.detail: Spend your last evening relaxing in your hotel room. Check your packing, reflect on your trip’s memories, and prepare for your departure tomorrow.

localTips: Visit Cascais for a beach day if the weather is nice.
Try the seafood in the Cais do Sodré area for dinner.
Save time for last-minute souvenir shopping in Chiado.

urgent.label: Book Day Trip to Sintra
urgent.note: Popular site; may need advance booking
